name: Hakim ibn Tufayl
title: Shameless Looter
shortDescription: One of the Kufan army's executioners who struck Abbas's (AS) left hand and looted his weapons and clothes after his martyrdom.
fullStory:
Hakim ibn Tufayl al-Ta'i was one of the hard-hearted commanders and fighters of Umar ibn Sa'd's army. He held a strange grudge against the Bani Hashem.

On the day of Ashura, when Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas (AS) went to the Euphrates to fetch water and was ambushed by dozens on his way back, Hakim ibn Tufayl hid behind a palm tree. After Abbas's right hand was severed, Hakim struck a heavy blow and severed the left hand of Karbala's standard-bearer as well.

His crimes didn't end there; after Abbas's (AS) martyrdom, he shamelessly looted his weapons and clothes. He was also among those who shot arrows at Imam Hussain (AS).

Hakim ibn Tufayl was captured after Mukhtar al-Thaqafi's uprising. His family went to Adi ibn Hatim al-Ta'i to intercede for him, but before the intercession letter arrived, Mukhtar's forces executed him by firing squad (with arrows) due to his horrific crimes.
quote: (His false and shameful boast:) I am the killer of Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas and I severed his left hand.
